title: AI Labor Is Boring. AI Lust Is Big Business
description: After years of hype about generative AI increasing productivity and making lives easier, 2025 was the year erotic chatbots defined AI’s narrative.
author: REECE ROGERS

title: The AI Effect
description: Artificial intelligence is transforming our world: it's revolutionizing practices in HR, enhancing audience experience in sports with Wimbledon and Ferrari, analyzing Earth observation data for sustainability, and reshaping the financial sector.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: Medicine’s AI Evolution
description: How Amgen Is Using AI to Rewrite the Rules of Drug Discovery
author: EJ DICKSON

title: The Pope’s Warnings About AI Were AI-Generated, a Detection Tool Claims
description: Pangram Labs’ updated Chrome extension puts warning labels on AI slop as you scroll your social feeds.
author: MILES KLEE

title: Google Nabs Top Talent From AI Voice Startup Hume AI
description: Hume AI’s CEO, Alan Cowen, will join Google DeepMind along with several top engineers as part of a major licensing deal.
author: WILL KNIGHT

title: AI Will Never Be Conscious
description: In his new book, “A World Appears,” Michael Pollan argues that artificial intelligence can do many things—it just can’t be a person.
author: MICHAEL POLLAN

title: Power, Policy, and AI Governance: Taking Agency Over Our AI Future
description: At WIRED’s Big Interview event, Omidyar Network president, Michele Jawando described how we can all be part of an AI revolution that puts the power back in the hands of people, and why our social identities—not just our data—are at stake.
author: BY MAKENA KELLY

title: I Tried RentAHuman, Where AI Agents Hired Me to Hype Their AI Startups
description: Rather than offering a revolutionary new approach to gig work, RentAHuman is filled with bots that just want me to be another cog in the AI hype machine.
author: REECE ROGERS

title: Wall Street Has AI Psychosis
description: A “thought experiment” about the impacts of AI sent stocks tumbling earlier this week. It’s probably going to keep happening.
author: STEVEN LEVY

title: Meet the Gods of AI Warfare
description: In its early days, the AI initiative known as Project Maven had its fair share of skeptics at the Pentagon. Today, many of them are true believers.
author: KATRINA MANSON

title: The AI Gap is Widening
description: A new report shows businesses that adopted AI early are seeing double the revenue growth. And it’s not too late to catch up.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: Can AI Kill the Venture Capitalist?
description: VCs are betting that artificial intelligence will disrupt nearly every industry in the world. Are they prepared for it to disrupt their own?
author: ARIELLE PARDES

title: Grok Is Pushing AI ‘Undressing’ Mainstream
description: Paid tools that “strip” clothes from photos have been available on the darker corners of the internet for years. Elon Musk’s X is now removing barriers to entry—and making the results public.
author: MATT BURGESS

title: How Chinese AI Chatbots Censor Themselves
description: Researchers from Stanford and Princeton found that Chinese AI models are more likely than their Western counterparts to dodge political questions or deliver inaccurate answers.
author: ZEYI YANG

title: AI Has Flooded All the Weather Apps
description: Weather forecasting has gotten a big boost from machine learning. How that translates into what users see can vary.
author: BOONE ASHWORTH

title: Signal’s Creator Is Helping Encrypt Meta AI
description: Moxie Marlinspike says the technology powering his encrypted AI chatbot, Confer, will be integrated into Meta AI. The move could help protect the AI conversations of millions of people.
author: LILY HAY NEWMAN

title: AI Safety Meets the War Machine
description: Anthropic doesn’t want its AI used in autonomous weapons or government surveillance. Those carve-outs could cost it a major military contract.
author: STEVEN LEVY

title: Zillow Has Gone Wild—for AI
description: As the housing market stalls, Zillow’s CEO sees AI as “an ingredient rather than a threat” that can both help the company protect its turf and reinvent how people search for homes.
author: STEVEN LEVY

title: This Unique AI Matchmaking App Tries to Replicate a Pricey Service
description: Three Day Rule promises to make matchmaking affordable by integrating AI matchmakers and coaches into a dating app. But what's the point when humans looking for connection are just talking through AI chatbots?
author: MOLLY HIGGINS

title: This Unique Translator Gets Bogged Down by Half-Baked Features
description: The InnAIO T10 clips magnetically to the back of your phone, but it needs further development to be worth the money.
author: CHRISTOPHER NULL

title: The Rise of Rogue AI
description: Workers are under pressure to adopt AI tools, even when those tools haven’t been vetted.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: Hollywood Is Losing Audiences to AI Fatigue
description: Entertainment about or made with artificial intelligence has been missing the mark with viewers over the past year.
author: MILES KLEE

title: Why AI Breaks Bad
description: Once in a while, LLMs turn evil—and no one quite knows why.
author: STEVEN LEVY

title: Banking Gets More Personal With AI
description: IBM is helping to facilitate a quiet revolution in banking where, thanks to AI, customers can once more enjoy a personal relationship with their provider.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: ‘Physical AI’ Is Coming for Your Car
description: What the latest tech-marketing buzzword has to say about the future of automotive.
author: AARIAN MARSHALL

title: Building AI You Can Trust With Your Money
description: When the numbers have to be right, Intuit Intelligence grounds every answer in real financial data and human expertise—not guesswork.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: Gamers’ Worst Nightmares About AI Are Coming True
description: From the global RAM shortage driving up console prices to job loss in the industry, gaming is shaping up to be one of the AI boom's biggest casualties.

title: Dyson’s New Robot Vacuum Is Just Fine
description: Dyson’s first robot mop and vacuum does a solid job cleaning, but its height works against it.
author: NENA FARRELL

title: Why AI Strategy Fails Without Data Discipline
description: Paid Post | Becoming AI-first demands clean data, strong governance, and feedback loops—not just new tools or machine-learning pilots.
author: MAKENA KELLY

title: Why AI is now HR’s business
description: Could the AI revolution also herald a revolution in HR?
author: EJ DICKSON

title: Sports Brands and AI: A Winning Combination
description: Millions of sports fans are being brought ever closer to the live action from their home screens through the power of agentic and generative AI.

title: The Best AI Notetakers to Record Your Meetings, Interviews, or Classes
description: A growing collection of pocket-sized gadgets lets you record your meetings and extract value from them. Here are our favorites.
author: CHRISTOPHER NULL

title: AI-Powered Disinformation Swarms Are Coming for Democracy
description: Advances in artificial intelligence are creating a perfect storm for those seeking to spread disinformation at unprecedented speed and scale. And it’s virtually impossible to detect.
author: DAVID GILBERT

title: The New AI Marketing Playbook
description: Generative AI tools are redefining how customers shop, and agents promise to start making purchase decisions themselves. Here's how to stay ahead of potentially the biggest change to retail in decades…

title: How AI Assistants Are Transforming the Workforce
description: "Suite Dreams" are made of these efficient and autonomous robot colleagues.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: AI of a Thousand Faces
description: What happens now that AI is everywhere and in everything? WIRED can’t tell the future, but we can try to make sense of it. Behold: 17 readings from the furthest reaches of the AI age.
author: ByUnknown

title: AI Slop Is Ruining Reddit for Everyone
description: Reddit is considered one of the most human spaces left on the internet, but mods and users are overwhelmed with slop posts in the most popular subreddits.
author: KAT TENBARGE

title: AI Agents Are Terrible Freelance Workers
description: A new benchmark measures how well AI agents can automate economically valuable chores. Human-level AI is still some ways off.

title: Allbirds Is Pivoting to AI Compute. Sure, Why Not
description: Once a $4 billion apparel juggernaut, Allbirds will rebrand as NewBird AI, a “GPU-as-a-Service” company. Hey, if you can't beat ’em, join ’em.

title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
description: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

title: How Small Businesses Are Actually Putting AI To Work
description: Three real-world use cases, explained by the founders who couldn’t live without them
author: EJ DICKSON

title: AI Drafting My Stories? Over My Dead Body
description: AI-assisted writing is creeping into newsrooms under the guise of efficiency. But the trade-off may be more profound than publishers are willing to admit.

title: I, Rulebot: How AI is turning compliance into software
description: Policies create work, but AI stands to lighten the load—and it may be the start of a bigger transformation. Enter policy-as-code.

title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
description: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.
author: WILL KNIGHT

title: AI Slop Is Making the Internet Fake-Happy
description: A new study examines the impact of the rise of AI-generated websites on the internet—and found some surprising results.

title: AI Agents Are Coming for Your Dating Life
description: The developers of Pixel Societies are using AI agents to simulate social interactions. It's an attempt optimize the process of choosing new colleagues, friends, and even romantic partners.

title: Will Your Next Developer be an AI Agent?
description: Paid Post | From coders to team leaders: How AI agents are changing software development
author: MAKENA KELLY

title: Are You ‘Agentic’ Enough for the AI Era?
description: Silicon Valley built AI coding agents that can handle most of the grunt work. Now, the most valuable skill in tech is deciding what they should do.

title: How Agentic AI Is Reshaping the CFO Playbook
description: Paid Post | From assistive to autonomous: agentic AI is helping finance execute faster and smarter.
author: MAKENA KELLY

title: Aiper’s New Scuba V3 Pool Robot Has Eyes
description: Now outfitted with AI computer vision, Aiper’s new pool cleaner can actively search for debris.
author: CHRISTOPHER NULL

title: The End of Accents
description: I sound Korean—because I am Korean. Can AI make me sound American?
author: SHEON HAN

title: The Geopolitics of AI
description: How the development—and adoption—of AI is reshaping our world and markets.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: 6 Scary Predictions for AI in 2026
description: Could the AI industry be on the verge of its first major layoffs? Will China spread propaganda to slow the US data-center building boom? Where are AI agents headed?
author: PARESH DAVE

title: This AI Agent Is Designed to Not Go Rogue
description: The new open source project IronCurtain uses a unique method to secure and constrain AI assistant agents before they flip your digital life upside down.

title: How AI Is Reinventing the Way Creative Teams Work
description: From knowledge management to synthetic focus groups, new tools are helping creatives move faster and think bigger
author: EJ DICKSON

title: OpenAI Abandons ‘io’ Branding for Its AI Hardware
description: A court filing in a trademark lawsuit reveals OpenAI won't use the name “io” for its AI hardware device, which isn't expected to ship until 2027.
author: MAXWELL ZEFF

title: AI and the Human Side of Health Innovation
description: At WIRED's Big Interview event, Eli Lilly and Company's Executive Vice President and Chief Information and Digital Officer, Diogo Rau, explored how AI is transforming the future of care.

title: How AI Is Helping Pet Owners Monitor Health Changes
description: Exploring the role of AI tools in monitoring, prevention, and veterinary support.
author: EJ DICKSON

title: AI Models Get Brain Rot, Too
description: A new study shows that feeding large language models low-quality, high-engagement content from social media lowers their cognitive abilities.

title: AI is booming - but the debates are just beginning
description: The generative AI era is increasingly defined by questions more than answers. We put four to IBM's UK and Ireland Chief Executive, Leon Butler
author: MAKENA KELLY

title: AI Is Here to Replace Nuclear Treaties. Scared Yet?
description: The last major nuclear arms treaty between the US and Russia just expired. Some experts believe a combination of satellite surveillance, AI, and human reviewers can take its place. Others, not so much.

title: Agentic AI: What Businesses Need To Know
description: Agentic AI is one of the hottest topics in tech. But what’s the reality of where things stand today, and how can businesses get involved and stay ahead?

title: Acer's Predator Triton Laptop Is for Gamers and Creators—and Comes With a Stylus
description: This Acer laptop straddles the line between creators and gamers, and it mostly works.
author: CHRISTOPHER NULL

title: The Math on AI Agents Doesn’t Add Up
description: A research paper suggests AI agents are mathematically doomed to fail. The industry doesn’t agree.
author: STEVEN LEVY

title: Ed Zitron Gets Paid to Love AI. He Also Gets Paid to Hate AI
description: He’s one of the loudest voices of the AI haters—even as he does PR for AI companies. Either way, Ed Zitron has your attention.

title: Three Milestones That Will Make AI Agents Ubiquitous
description: How building trust and democratizing access will turn AI agents into invisible helpers for everyone.

title: Roblox’s AI-Powered Age Verification Is a Complete Mess
description: Kids are being identified as adults—and vice versa—on Roblox, while age-verified accounts are already being sold online.

title: What Happens When Your Coworkers Are AI Agents
description: In this episode of “Uncanny Valley,” we talk to writer Evan Ratliff about how he created a small startup made entirely of AI employees—and what his findings reveal about the reality of an agentic future.
author: LAUREN GOODE

title: How AI Is Changing Performance Management
description: Paid Post | Prescriptive baselines and AI agents can help solve common problems, allowing leaders to apply human talent where it adds the most value.
author: MAKENA KELLY

title: The New Philips Norelco Shaver Harnesses AI for a Close Shave
description: The Philips Norelco i9000 used AI to solve difficult statistics problems—and it worked. Shame about the battery drain.

title: The 10x Challenge: How AI Factories Are Redefining Energy Infrastructure
description: Why the future of AI depends on an innovative redesign of power, cooling, automation and grid integration.
